text: Kutaa bíi wúntée ní ituun in bí Looti ceeyee bíi páb́-u ki lì yii: «Yīkì kí yòon̄ a-nimpuu nì a-bisalib liitil nee kí kpáàá nyā kí taa yàntī ditafadaaldi din làá jēetí kitiŋ nee puee ń kpáaǹ-si.»

ref: At first, Lot just stood there. But the Lord wanted to save him. So the angels took Lot, his wife, and his two daughters by the hand and led them out of the city.
text: Ama ní Looti bí ki tàantí kaa làá nyā malaa. Ní bininjab bilee cúú u nì u-nimpuu nì u-bisalib bilee ŋalii ki dáaĺ-bi ki nyántì kitiŋ kpaanceetiŋu. Di sá yii ti-Dindaan nìn là kí fīī-u ní.

ref: When they were outside, one of the angels said, “Run for your lives! Don't even look back. And don't stop in the valley. Run to the hills, where you'll be safe.”
text: Bi dáaĺ-bi ki nyántèe ní bi-kansikin ubɔ bí Looti yii: «Sāǹ kí fīī a-ba! A yaa bíi sáńee, á taa fàtī kí kpèè a-boon, kí taa yūĺ laaba. A yaa kaa là ŋkúmee, á nīn pée sáń ki cá ajoon watil pú.»

ref: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: Tiŋman, man a-tɔntɔnliu ká diyimɔɔl a-cee. A nín fīī-mi ŋkúminee, a dàkā-mi kulaŋŋaŋfitiiu tikpil. Ama m yaa bí yii m làá nīn sáń ki cá ajoon watil púee, maa làá bà ní mbusu nee ń dāá jēetí m-pu ní ḿ kpú.

ref: There's a town near here. It's only a small place, but my family and I will be safe, if you let us go there.”
text: M gbāam̀-si, kpèè kitimbilii kiba di bí m-nyɔkɔpu nee, kaa dátì ḿ ŋmā kí sāǹ kí bà niin. Kitiŋ gbanti kaa náaǹaa! Yàntī ḿ sāǹ kí kɔ́ ki-ni kí ŋmátí.»

ref: “All right, go there,” he answered. “I won't destroy that town.
text: Ní u kíí Looti yii: «M làá gíti pɔ̄-si diyimɔɔl. A-puee maa làá kúntí kitimbilii kin bɔti a sòoǹ nee.

ref: Hurry! Run! I can't do anything until you are safely there.” The town was later called Zoar because Lot had said it was small. Sodom and Gomorrah Are Destroyed
text: Malaa sāǹ kí báĺ niin, kun puee a yaa kaa láá bàn̄ kitiŋ gbantin, maa ŋūǹ kí ŋá tiba.» An pu ní bi pú kitiŋ gbanti diyindi yii Soowaar.

ref: The sun was coming up as Lot reached the town of Zoar,
text: Ŋwìiń bíi nyēēyee ní Looti bàn̄ Soowaar timbiliikin.

ref: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
text: Niinee ní ti-Dindaan yāntì farwata nyánní yilpu ki fōō ŋŋmi ki sìtī kii utaayee Soodoom nì Goomoora timmu pu.

ref: He destroyed those cities and everyone who lived in them, as well as their land and the trees and grass that grew there.
text: U nìn nāaǹ ntimu gbanti nì ditantakal din poon mu bée kɔkɔ, ki kūū ki céetì mu-nib nì isufi nì timool kɔkɔ.

ref: On the way, Lot's wife looked back and was turned into a block of salt.
text: Ní Looti nimpuu fātì ki kpēē u-boon ní ki kpántì diyataŋkpal ki yúl̀.

ref: That same morning Abraham got up and went to the place where he had stood and spoken with the Lord.
text: Ku wúntì kutaafaau pɔ̀lpɔlee, Abraam péé ki cūtì laakin u fu yúl̀ ti-Dindaan cee ki sòon̄ nì uee.

ref: He looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and saw smoke rising from all over the land—it was like a flaming furnace.
text: U kpēē Soodoom nì Goomoora timmu nì ditantakal din poon mu bée watil púee, ki ká ŋŋminyiim nyēē kitiŋ ki dùkù ki jɔ́ḿ, a lì kii kipaai nyiimee.

ref: When God destroyed the cities of the valley where Lot lived, he remembered his promise to Abraham and saved Lot from the terrible destruction. Moab and Ammon
text: Ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti nìn kúntì ntimu mun bí ditantakal poonee, waa nìn súntì Abraam pu ní ki fīī Looti ki nyántì kitiŋ kin poon u nìn kóòyee ki wāatì ní ki nín nāaǹ-kì. Moowaab nì Amɔn yab nyeeŋu

ref: Lot was afraid to stay on in Zoar. So he took his two daughters and moved to a cave in the hill country.
text: Looti nìn fàŋkí kí nīn kóò Soowaar tiŋkin, an puee ní u kpáaǹ u-bisalib liitil ki yíkì ki tin kóòń ajoon pu itaŋkpalɔkɔn.

ref: One day his older daughter said to her sister, “Our father is old, and there are no men anywhere for us to marry.
text: Dalbee ní ukpil bí u-naal yii: «Ti-baa púĺ, ní bininjab mun kpá doo tí mɔ̄ǹ-bi kii an nín pée sá báà ŋma mɔ́ń puee.

ref: Let's get our father drunk! Then we can sleep with him and have children.”
text: Yàntī tí nyùntī ti-baa ndaam, kí dòon̄ u-cee kí màal̀-u mbiyaamu ú ká inibool.»

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Kujoou gbantee ní bi nyūntì bi-baa ndaam ní u gbí, ní ukpil dōoǹ u-baa cee kunyeeu gbanti. U-baa kaa nìn kpáàa béè ŋyunti ŋun u kóoń ki dōoǹee nì ŋyunti ŋun u yíkèe gba.

ref: The next day the older daughter said to her sister, “I slept with my father last night. We'll get him drunk again tonight, so you can sleep with him, and we can each have a child.”
text: Kutaa wúntèe ní ukpil tùkū u-naal yii: «M fu dōoǹ ti-baa cee kunyeeu. Tí gíti nyùntī-u ndaam kunyeeu nee si mun ń cùú dòon̄ u-cee, tí ŋmā kí tīī ti-baa inibool.»

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and this time the younger sister slept with him. But once again he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Ní bi tí nyūntì bi-baa ndaam kunyeeu gbanti, ní kiwaayee mun cūtì ki dōoǹ bi-baa cee, ní bi-baa kaa kpáàa béè ŋyunti ŋun u kóoń ki dōoǹee nì ŋyunti ŋun u yíkèe gba.

ref: That's how Lot's two daughters had children.
text: Niinee, Looti bisalib liitilee dūu cáá u-pool.

ref: The older daughter named her son Moab, and he is the ancestor of the Moabites.
text: Ní ukpil màl̄ kibininjabiki ní ki pú-kì diyindi yii Moowaab. Úǹ di sá díǹ pu nee Moowaab yab naanja.

ref: The younger daughter named her son Benammi, and he is the ancestor of the Ammonites.
text: Ní u-naal mun màl̄ kibininjabiki ní ki pú-kì diyindi yii Been-Amii. Úǹ di sá díǹ pu nee Amɔn yab naanja.

ref: he told everyone that his wife Sarah was his sister. So King Abimelech of Gerar had Sarah brought to him.
text: Abraam nìn lī binib cee yii u-nimpuu Saara sá u-niisal ní. An pu ní Geeraar bɔtiu Abimeeleeki tú bi cúú Saara ki cūnnì-u.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Kunyeeuee ní Unimbɔti dɔ́miń Abimeeleeki cee kudamintiiu poon ní ki bí-u yii: «A làá kpú unimpu un a fōō ki làá cáá nee pu, kun puee u cáá cal.»

ref: Abimelech said to the Lord, “Don't kill me! I haven't slept with Sarah. Didn't they say they were brother and sister? I am completely innocent.”
text: Abraam u-ba di tùkū-mi yii u-niisal dee, ní unimpu u-ba mun lī yii Abraam sá u-niija. An puee tin man ŋá nee, m ŋá nì nlandɔkɔmɔnti ní, maa yīntì.»

ref: Then God continued: I know you are innocent. That's why I kept you from sleeping with Sarah and doing anything wrong.
text: U-damintiiku kubaantiiu gbanti poonee ní Unimbɔti bí-u yii: «Man m-ba nyí yii an sá nì nlandɔkɔmɔnti ní a ŋá míǹ. Man di cúú-si á taa yìntīǹ-mi, an pu ní maa yāntì a gbēē-u.

ref: Her husband is a prophet. Let her go back to him, and his prayers will save you from death. But if you don't return her, you and all your people will die.
text: Kɔŋkɔnnee, gíiǹ unimpuee kí tīī u-cal, u sá m-bɔnaatiliu ní, u làá mèè kí tīī-si á taa kpú. Ama a yaa kaa gíiǹ u-nimpuu ki tīī-u, á nīn nyí yii sii nì a-yab kɔkɔ làá kpú.»

ref: Early the next morning Abimelech sent for his officials, and when he told them what had happened, they were frightened.
text: Abimeeleeki fīntì kutaafaau pɔ̀lpɔlee ní ki yíiń u-tɔntɔnliibi kɔkɔ ní ki dū an bɔtee ki máafì-bi, ní tifaandi cúú-bi tikpil.

ref: Abimelech then called in Abraham and said: Look what you've done to us! What have I ever done to you? Why did you make me and my nation guilty of such a terrible sin?
text: Ní Abimeeleeki yíiń Abraam ní ki bālifì-u yii: «Ba ŋá ní a ŋá-ti mimmee? Tilati ní m yīntiǹ-si ní a bàà làá dū man nì m-beelin yab kí kɔ́ǹ ikpitikpaan poon míǹ? A ŋá-mi digaŋgam un kaa máaǹ bí ŋá unilee.»

ref: What were you thinking when you did this?
text: Ní ki gíti bí yii: «Ba di yāntì ní a ŋá míǹ?»

ref: Abraham answered: I did it because I didn't think any of you respected God, and I was sure that someone would kill me to get my wife.
text: Ní Abraam kíí-u yii: «M dākafì yii doo nib kaa fàŋkí Unimbɔti, bi yaa ká m-nimpuu, bi ŋūǹ ki kpɔ̀-mi kí fōō-u.

ref: Besides, she is my half sister. We have the same father, but different mothers.
text: Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, u baasii sá m-niisal ní. Di sá man nì u pēē baa ní kaa pēē na, an pu ní m ŋúń ki cáá-u.

ref: When God made us leave my father's home and start wandering, I told her, “If you really love me, then tell everyone that I am your brother.”
text: Unimbɔti nín yāntì m yíkì m-tiŋkin m-baa cee kí bíi yìiǹee, m tùkū m-nimpuu yii: <Min ní a làá dàkà yii a néeń-mee di sá laakin kamaa ní ti yaa bàn̄ee, á nīn yíì-mi yii a-niija.>»

ref: After Abimelech had given Abraham some sheep, cattle, and slaves, he sent Sarah back
text: Niinee ní Abimeeleeki cúú inaa nì ipii nì iŋoobu nì iyumbu bininjab nì binimpoobi ki tīī-u, ní ki gíiǹ u-nimpuu Saara mun ki tīī-u.

ref: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.
text: Ní ki bí-u yii: «Kpèè, m-tiŋki di dɔ̄ a-nimbiin nee, lēē laakin ní a lèe kí nīn kóò.»

ref: Meanwhile, God had kept Abimelech's wife and slaves from having children. But Abraham prayed, and God let them start having children again.
text: Di sá yii tin nìn jítì Abraam nimpuu Saara puee yāntì ti-Dindaan pīiǹ Abimeeleeki nì u-dicaŋku yab kɔkɔ maaliŋu.

ref: The Lord was good to Sarah and kept his promise.
text: Ŋyunti bāań ní ti-Dindaan bīiǹ Saara boon ní ki lafun ŋá min u nìn pōoǹee ki tīī-u.

ref: Although Abraham was very old, Sarah had a son exactly at the time God had said.
text: Nì min kɔkɔ Abraam nìn púĺ tikpilee, Saara cáá dipool ki màl̄-u kibininjabiki ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti nìn pée yóoǹ Abraamee deedee.

ref: Abraham named his son Isaac,
text: Ní Abraam pú kibiki kin Saara màl̄-uee diyindi yii Isaaki.

ref: and when the boy was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, just as God had commanded.
text: Kibikee ŋá iwiin iniiyee ní Abraam gíí-kì dikpancandi kii Unimbɔti nín nìn tùkū-u yii ú ŋá puee.

ref: and Sarah said, “God has made me laugh. And now everyone will laugh with me.
text: Ní Saara bí yii: «Unimbɔti yāntì m láń! Míǹ ní bin kɔkɔ làá gbìl̄ m-bɔti neeyee mun làá lā.»

ref: Who would have dared to tell Abraham that someday I would have a child? But in his old age, I have given him a son.”
text: Ní ki gíti bí yii: «Ŋma di nìn ŋūǹ kí lì Abraam cee yii man Saara dāa bí kí ŋmàb̄ kibiki? Ama nì míǹ kɔkɔee, m màl̄-u kibininjabiki u-puti poon!» Bi jìn̄ Agaar nì u-biki Isimaayeel

ref: The time came when Sarah no longer had to nurse Isaac, and on that day Abraham gave a big feast. Hagar and Ishmael Are Sent Away
text: Isaaki cíkitì, ní bi gíí-u dibiil. Ŋwiin ŋun bi gíí-u dibiilee ní Abraam yāntì bi jíń jiŋaakpaan ubɔ.

ref: One day, Sarah noticed Hagar's son Ishmael playing, and she said to Abraham, “Get rid of that Egyptian slave woman and her son! I don't want him to inherit anything. It should all go to my son.”
text: Ní Saara bí Abraam yii: «Jà uyumbu nee nì u-biki. Di sá yii u-biki nì man biki Isaaki kaa máaǹ ki kpāfì kí jī a-fakal.»

ref: But God said, “Abraham, don't worry about your slave woman and the boy. Just do what Sarah tells you. Isaac will inherit your family name,
text: Ní Unimbɔti bí-u yii: «A-jipɔɔn Isimaayeel nì u-na bɔti ń taa wà-si. Gbìl̄ kí tīiǹ Saara, kun puee an sá Isaaki di làá dāá màal̀ a-naantiibi bin làá dāá fōō a-yindee.

ref: but the son of the slave woman is also your son, and I will make his descendants into a great nation.”
text: Ama, m làá yàntī uyumbu bikee mun ń dāá kpántí ditimbuŋkpaandi baa, kun puee u sá a-maaliŋun ní.»

ref: Early the next morning Abraham gave Hagar an animal skin full of water and some bread. Then he put the boy on her shoulder and sent them away. They wandered around in the desert near Beersheba,
text: Kutaafaau pɔ̀lpɔlee ní Abraam yíkì ki nyàab̄ kpɔnɔb, ní ki dū nnyim ki gbéeǹ kuloou ki dūu būkuǹ Agaar, ní ki nyántì u nì u-biki. Agaar búntì ki tú ki yìiǹ kuteeu pu Beerseeba jandi pú.

ref: and after they had run out of water, Hagar put her son under a bush.
text: Nnyim dāa dóò kuloou poonee ní Agaar cáá u-biki ki bíl̀ kisubiki kiba taapu mboonni.

ref: Then she sat down a long way off, because she could not bear to watch him die. And she cried bitterly.
text: Ní ki sútì ki cūtìi kāl̀ ŋkpaanceeti, kun puee u lī u-neen yii: «Maa là kí ká kibiki nee nín làá kpú puee.» An puee u kāń ndan ní ki sūǹ ki yīkintí.

ref: When God heard the boy crying, the angel of God called out to Hagar from heaven and said, “Hagar, why are you worried? Don't be afraid. I have heard your son crying.
text: Niinee Unimbɔti gbìl̄ kibikee bíi sūǹ, ní u-tuuŋŋu kālìń yilpu ki yíiń Agaar ki bí-u yii: «Agaar, ba kpá-si ní? Taa fàŋkì tiba, kun puee laakin a-biki bí nee, ki-siim bàn̄ Unimbɔti.

ref: Help him up and hold his hand, because I will make him the father of a great nation.”
text: Yīkì kí cùú yòon̄ kibiki kí nīn ŋúb́-kì tiŋan, kun puee m làá yàntī kí dāá nīn sá ditimbuŋkpaandi baa ní.»

ref: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.
text: Ní Unimbɔti yāntì u-nimbiliŋ ŋāatì ní u wál̀ dinyimbɔbil nì nnyim, ní ki cūtì ki lúń ki gbéeǹ kuloou ní ki cáań ki nyūntì u-biki.

ref: God blessed Ishmael, and as the boy grew older, he became an expert at hunting with his bow and arrows. He lived in the Paran Desert, and his mother chose an Egyptian woman for him to marry. A Peace Treaty
text: U nìn kóò kupɔɔu kun nìn bí Paaran tiŋkinee ní u-na dāa cáá unimpu Eejipiti ki tīī-u. Abraam nì Abimeeleeki pūtì ki pōoǹ tɔb

ref: About this time Abimelech and his army commander Phicol said to Abraham, “God blesses everything you do!
text: Ŋyunti gbantee ní Abimeeleeki nì u-janjakpiliu Piikool dɔ́miń Abraam cee ní Abimeeleeki bí-u yii: «Ti ká yii tin kɔkɔ a bí ki ŋáańee, Unimbɔti bí a-cee.

ref: Now I want you to promise in the name of God that you will always be loyal to me and my descendants, just as I have always been loyal to you in this land where you have lived as a foreigner.”
text: Míǹ puee pùtī nì Unimbɔti doo nee yii aa làá nyā man nì m-biyaamu nì m-naantiibi boon, ní ki làá ŋá man nì m-tiŋki kin poon a kóò nee tiŋan kii ti mun nín ŋá-si tiŋan puee.»

ref: And so, Abraham promised he would.
text: Ní Abraam kíí-u yii: «M pūtì yii m làá ŋá míǹ».

ref: One day, Abraham told Abimelech, “Some of your servants have taken over one of my wells.”
text: Ama ní Abraam dū lɔkɔ un u gbìn̄ ní Abimeeleeki tɔntɔnliibi ŋá mpɔn ki fōōyee bɔti ki bíl̀ u-nimbiin.

ref: “This is the first I've heard about it,” Abimelech replied. “Why haven't you said something before? I don't have any idea who did it.”
text: Ní Abimeeleeki bí-u yii: «Maa nyí un di túń an tundee. Si mun a-ba kaa lī tibɔti gbanti m-cee, maa tí gbìl̄-tì ubɔ cee dalba, see díǹ.»

ref: Abraham gave Abimelech some sheep and cattle, then the two men made a peace treaty.
text: Niinee Abraam cúú ipii nì iŋoobu nì inaa ki tīī Abimeeleeki, ní bi-liitil pūtì ki pōoǹ tɔb.

ref: Abraham separated seven female lambs from his flock of sheep,
text: Ní Abraam gāntì ipisal iluli ki yóoǹ.

ref: and Abimelech asked, “Why have you done this?”
text: Abimeeleeki ká mimmee ní ki bālifì-u yii: «Ba pu ní a gāntì ipisal iluli nee?»

ref: Abraham replied, “I want you to accept these seven lambs as proof that I dug this well.”
text: Ní Abraam kíí-u yii: «M gāntì-i ki síiǹ kí tīī-si án nīn sá kudaaŋŋu yii man di gbìn̄ lɔkɔ nee.»

ref: So they called the place Beersheba, because they made a treaty there.
text: An pu ní bi yíì mpaan gbanti yii Beerseeba, kun puee niin ní bi-liitil nìn pūtì.

ref: When the treaty was completed, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol went back to the land of the Philistines.
text: Bi nín pūtì ki pōoǹ tɔb Beerseeba niinee, ní Abimeeleeki nì u-janjakpiliu Piikool fātìi nín kùǹ bi-dumpu Fiiliistii yab tiŋkin.

ref: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and worshiped the eternal Lord God.
text: Ní Abraam cóoǹ busubu bun bi yíì yii tamariisee Beerseeba ní ki jáam̀ ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti un pée bí kí nīn cá jaanjaanee.

ref: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.
text: Abraam nìn kāl̀ Fiiliistii yab tiŋkin ki yúntì tikpil.

ref: Some years later God decided to test Abraham, so he spoke to him. Abraham answered, “Here I am, Lord.”
text: Míǹ boonee, Unimbɔti cɔ́ŋkì Abraam. U nìn yíiń-u yii: «Abraam!» Ní Abraam kíí yii: «M sèé!»

ref: The Lord said, “Go get Isaac, your only son, the one you dearly love! Take him to the land of Moriah, and I will show you a mountain where you must sacrifice him to me on the fires of an altar.”
text: Ní Unimbɔti bí-u yii: «Yòon̄ a-jipɔmbaabil Isaaki un a néeńee kí nīn cá Mooriya tiŋkin. A yaa bàn̄ niinee, á dū-u kí ŋá saraawɔkaa. M làá dàkà-si dijool din pu a làá ŋá-mi saraa gbantee.»

ref: So Abraham got up early the next morning and chopped wood for the fire. He put a saddle on his donkey and set out with Isaac and two servants for the place where God had told him to go.
text: Abraam yíkì kutaafaau pɔ̀lpɔlee ki kpàl̄ u-maŋki ní ki yōoǹ u-tɔntɔnliibi bili ki kútì u-jipɔɔn Isaaki pu, ní ki látì idɔ in bi làá tin dūú ŋá saraawɔkaayee. Bi bɔ́ntì míǹ ki dóòyee ní ki búntì laakin Unimbɔti bí yii bí cùee.

ref: Three days later Abraham looked off in the distance and saw the place.
text: Bi búntì míǹ, winta dalee, Abraam kpēē u-nyɔkɔpuee, ní ki wál̀ laakin Unimbɔti bí yii bí cùee pánní ndan.

ref: He told his servants, “Stay here with the donkey, while my son and I go over there to worship. We will come back.”
text: Ní u bí u-tɔntɔnliibi bilee yii: «Nimbi ń gbíntí doo nì kimaŋ, man nì m-jipɔɔn ń càatī lapu doo kí jāam̀ Unimbɔti kí fàtīń.»

ref: Abraham put the wood on Isaac's shoulder, but he carried the hot coals and the knife. As the two of them walked along,
text: Niinee Abraam fōō idɔ in u làá tin dūú kpāā kí ŋá saraawɔkaayee u-tɔntɔnliibi cee, ki dūu túǹ u-jipɔɔn Isaaki, ní ki ŋúb́ u-ba ŋŋmi nì kijiki, ní bi-liitil nóó ki nín cá.

ref: Isaac said, “Father, we have the coals and the wood, but where is the lamb for the sacrifice?” “My son,” Abraham answered, “God will provide the lamb.” The two of them walked on, and
text: Ní u-baa kíí-u yii: «M-biki, Unimbɔti u-ba di làá tin tīī-ti dipibil din ti làá dūú ŋá saraawɔkaayee.» Ní bi nín nìkì bi-saŋŋu.

ref: when they reached the place that God had told him about, Abraham built an altar and placed the wood on it. Next, he tied up his son and put him on the wood.
text: Bi bàn̄ laakin Unimbɔti dàkā-bi bí cùee, ní Abraam máń niin nsaraafan, ki kɔ́fì idɔ ki páaǹ ní ki bóob́ u-jipɔɔn Isaaki ki páaǹ idɔee pu.

ref: He then took the knife and got ready to kill his son.
text: Abraam ŋá míǹ ki dóòyee ní ki tāntì u-ŋal ki yōoǹ kijiki kí bàà làntì u-jipɔɔn.

ref: But the Lord's angel shouted from heaven, “Abraham! Abraham!” “Here I am!” he answered.
text: Kpalaayee ní ti-Dindaan tuuŋŋu téenní yilpu ní ki bí yii: «Abraam! Abraam!» Ní u kíí yii: «M sèé».

ref: “Don't hurt the boy or harm him in any way!” the angel said. “Now I know that you truly obey God, because you were willing to offer him your only son.”
text: Ní ntuunee bí-u yii: «Á taa dū kijiki kí gbēē kibikee ní kaa nín ŋá-kì tiba. Di sá yii kɔŋkɔnnee m béè yii a fàŋkí Unimbɔti, kaa yíì kí dū a-jipɔmbaabil kí ŋá saraawɔkaa kí tīī-u.»

ref: Abraham looked up and saw a ram caught by its horns in the bushes. So he took the ram and sacrificed it instead of his son.
text: Ntuun sòon̄ míǹ ki dóò, Abraam fātèe, upìja di yú u-yiŋyi lɔ̀kɔ̄ kisugbooyee, ní u cūtìi cúuń ki cáań ki kūū ki ŋá saraa ki téeǹ u-jipɔɔn yil pu.

ref: Abraham named that place “The Lord Will Provide.” And even now people say, “On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.”
text: Ní ki pú mpaan gbanti diyindi yii: «Ti-Dindaan di tìì.» An pu ní díǹ pu nee, bi lì yii: «Ti-Dindaan jool yilpu ní u tìì-ti kun lóò-tee.»

ref: The Lord's angel called out from heaven a second time:
text: Ní ti-Dindaan tuuŋŋu tí yíinní Abraam yilpu nliliitiim

ref: You were willing to offer your only son to the Lord, and so he makes you this solemn promise,
text: ki bí yii: «Ti-Dindaan nín lī pu ki síiǹee sèé: <Kii a nín kaa yíì ki bàà làá dū a-jipɔmbaabil kí ŋá saraa kii m nín bí yii á ŋá puee, m pūtì nì m-ba yii

ref: “I will bless you and give you such a large family, that someday your descendants will be more numerous than the stars in the sky or the grains of sand along the seashore. They will defeat their enemies and take over the cities where their enemies live.
text: m làá bìin̄ a-boon tikpil. M làá bùtī a-maaliŋu ŋú nīn wīikí kii iŋmalbijaa nín kpátì kutaa pu puee, kí nīn wīikí kii tiŋku gbandi bulee. A-naantiibi di làá nīn pée pɔ̄ɔǹ bi-nannanliibi.

ref: You have obeyed me, and so you and your descendants will be a blessing to all nations on earth.”
text: A-maaliŋu pu ní m làá dāá dī kí bìin̄ kitiŋ pu timbuŋŋi kɔkɔ nib boon, kun puee a pílifì m-nyɔkɔ.>»

ref: Abraham and Isaac went back to the servants who had come with him, and they returned to Abraham's home in Beersheba. The Children of Nahor
text: Míǹ boonee ní Abraam fātì ki nín gítiń u-tɔntɔnliibi cee. U bāań-bee ní bi-kɔkɔ nín fàtīń Beerseeba tiŋkin. Beerseeba tiŋki gbantin ní Abraam nìn kóò.

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: Naakɔɔr nìn gíti cáá ubɔnti ubɔ bi yíì-u yii Reewuma, u mun nìn màl̄-u mbininjabiyaamu. Bíǹ di sá Teeba nì Gaam nì Taasi nì Maaka.

ref: “I live as a foreigner in your land, and I don't own any property where I can bury my wife. Please let me buy a piece of land.”
text: «M sá unicaan ní ki kóò ni-kansikin doo. Ní pɔ̄maan-mi kuceeu ḿ síb́ m-nimpuu.»

ref: “Sir,” they answered, “you are an important man. Choose the best place to bury your wife. None of us would refuse you a resting place for your dead.”
text: «Pīlifiǹ-ti, ukpil! A sá Unimbɔti niliu ubɔ ní ki kóò ti-kansikin. Lēē ti-taŋkpiifalii poon ŋun pée ŋān̄ a-ceeyee kí síb́ a-nimpuu. Báà ubɔ ti-kansikin kaa làá yíí yii á taa síb́ a-niliu u-taŋkpiifaliŋu poon.»

ref: and replied, “If you are willing to let me bury my wife here, please ask Zohar's son Ephron
text: ní ki bí-bi yii: «Ni yaa lafun kíí tiŋman yii ḿ síb́ m-nimpuuee, ní gbàam̀ Soowaar jipɔɔn Eefrɔn,

ref: to sell me Machpelah Cave at the end of his field. I'll pay what it's worth, and all of you can be witnesses.”
text: ú tīī-mi u-taŋkpalɔkɔŋu ŋun bí Makipeelee. U-taŋkpalɔkɔŋu gbanti bí u-kpaabukuŋu. Ú dū-ŋu kí nyāfiǹ-mi ni-kɔkɔ nimbiin án kpántí mpaan ŋun m làá nīn síb̀ m-nib ni-kansikinee. M làá pà-u ŋu-nyampooku nín sá puee deedee.»

ref: Ephron was sitting there near the city gate, when Abraham made this request, and he answered,
text: Eefrɔn gbanti nìn kā u-nabiyaamu Eeti yab kansikin, ní ki nìn kíí Abraam, Eeti yab bin kɔkɔ nìn dɔ́miń ki kā bi-tiŋki punyɔkɔpuee nimbiin yii:

ref: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
text: «Aayee, ukpil! Pīlifiǹ-mi, maa làá nyāfiǹ-si kusaau nì ntaŋkpalɔkɔ ŋun bí ku-poonee. M làá dūú pɔ̄-si ní m-nabiyaamu kɔkɔ nimbiin, á cù kí síb́ a-nimpuu.»

ref: Once again, Abraham bowed down
text: Ní Abraam fātìi tí gbāaǹ binib gbanti nimbiin

ref: and said to Ephron, “In front of these witnesses, I offer you the full price, so I can bury my wife. Please accept my offer.”
text: ní ki bí Eefrɔn, samaa kɔkɔ nimbiin yii: «M gbāam̀-si, gbìl̄ kí tīiǹ-mi. Yàntī ḿ dā kusaau gbanti kí pà-si ku-daaku nín sá puee, á fōō ḿ ŋmā kí síb́ m-nimpuu niin.»

ref: So Abraham buried his wife Sarah in Machpelah Cave that was in the field
text: Míǹ boonee ní Abraam cáá u-nimpuu Saara ki síb́ ntaŋkpalɔkɔ gbanti poon, Makipeela saakun Mamiree caŋin, an dàkà yii Eebrɔn, Kanaan yab tiŋkin.

ref: Abraham was now a very old man. The Lord had made him rich, and he was successful in everything he did.
text: Abraam nìn púĺ tikpil, ní ti-Dindaan mun nìn bīiǹ u-boon ajan kɔkɔ pu.

ref: One day, Abraham called in his most trusted servant and said to him, “Solemnly promise me
text: Dalbee, Abraam yíiń u-tɔntɔnliu un yúntì u-cee ki sá ukpil ki kpèé u-wanti kɔkɔee ní ki bí-u yii: «Dū a-ŋal kí tíkiń m-tapandi taapu.

ref: in the name of the Lord, who rules heaven and earth, that you won't choose a wife for my son Isaac from the people here in the land of Canaan.
text: M là á pùtī ti-Dindaan yilpu nì taapu Nimbɔtiu yii aa làá cáá Kanaan yab bin kansikin m kóò nee sapɔmbi ubɔ kí tīī m-jipɔɔn.

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for him from among my relatives.”
text: Ama a làá cù m-dicaŋku poon ní kí cáá unimpu kí tīī m-jipɔɔn Isaaki.»

ref: But the servant asked, “What if the young woman I choose refuses to leave home and come here with me? Should I send Isaac there to look for a wife?”
text: Ní u-tɔntɔnliuee kíí-u yii: «An yaa yíì, unimpu kaa làá dāá kíí kí nóoń-mi kí dāan̄ kitiŋ kin poon ti bí nee. Mimmee ḿ dāá gíiǹ a-jipɔɔn a-tiŋki kin poon a yíkìńeeee?»

ref: “No!” Abraham answered. “Don't ever do that, no matter what.
text: Ní Abraam bí-u yii: «Aayee, á taa pée màkan̄ ŋwindal yii a làá gíiǹ m-jipɔɔn m-tiŋkin.

ref: The Lord who rules heaven brought me here from the land where I was born and promised that he would give this land to my descendants forever. When you go back there, the Lord will send his angel ahead of you to help you find a wife for my son.
text: Ti-Dindaan yilpu Nimbɔtiu un yōoǹní-mi m-tiŋkin nì m-baa cindinee di sòon̄ ki pūtì ki tùkū-mi yii: <M làá dāá dū kitiŋ kin poon a bí nee ní kí tīī a-naantiibi.> Unimbɔti gbanti di làá tɔ̄ń u-tuuŋŋu ní ŋú céeǹ-si niin ní á ká unimpu kí tīī m-jipɔɔn.

ref: If the woman refuses to come along, you don't have to keep this promise. But don't ever take my son back there.”
text: Ama unimpu yaa yíì yii waa làá nóó-si kí dāan̄ dooyee, mimmee a nyáń dipùtil din m yāntì a pūtèe bɔtin. Nì míǹ kɔkɔee, á taa gíiǹ m-jipɔɔn m-tiŋkin.»

ref: So the servant gave Abraham his word that he would do everything he had been told to do.
text: Bi sòon̄ míǹ ki dóòyee ní utɔntɔnlee dū u-ŋal ki tíkiǹ u-kpiliu Abraam tapandi taapu ní ki pūtì yii u làá ŋá míǹ.

ref: Soon after that, the servant loaded ten of Abraham's camels with valuable gifts. Then he set out for the city in northern Syria, where Abraham's brother Nahor lived.
text: Boonee ní utɔntɔnlee yōoǹ u-kpiliu gutaamui saalaa ní ki lēē u-kpiliu gaajawanti poon tiwammɔntiil tikpil, ní ki búntì Aram-Naarayiim, Naakɔɔr tiŋkin watil pú.

ref: When he got there, he let the camels rest near the well outside the city. It was late afternoon, the time when the women came out for water.
text: U bàn̄ee ní ki cáá igutaamu ki gbāaǹ dinyimbɔbil din bí ki pìlīǹ kitiŋinee caŋin. An nìn sá kujoou ŋyunti ŋun binimpoobi dòoń ki lúù nnyimee ní.

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Ní u mēē yii: «Ti-Dindaan, m-kpiliu Abraam Nimbɔtiu, m gbāam̀-si, dàkà m-kpiliu Abraam a-ŋanti kí yàntī ḿ ká kun m nyàab̀ee díǹ.

ref: The young women of the city will soon come to this well for water,
text: Kpèè, m yú dinyimbɔbil caŋin ní nee ní bisapɔmbi nyēeń kitiŋin ki dòoń ki lúù nnyim.

ref: and I'll ask one of them for a drink. If she gives me a drink and then offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen and that you have kept your promise to my master.
text: Usapɔɔn un m yaa bí yii: <M gbāam̀-si, kpòon̄ a-baŋki ḿ nyɔ̀,> ní u yaa bí-mi yii: <Nyɔ̀m̄ ḿ lúń kí tīī a-gutaamui mun ń nyɔ̀ee,> usapɔɔn gbanti ń nīn sá un a lēē kí tīī a-tɔntɔnliu Isaakee. A yaa ŋá mimmee ní m làá béé yii a ŋá m-kpiliu tiŋan.»

ref: Abraham's servant ran to her and said, “Please let me have a drink of water.”
text: Ní utɔntɔnlee sáǹ ki kíntì-u ní ki bí-u yii: «M gbāam̀-si, pɔ̄-mi a-nyim fiii ḿ nyɔ̀.»

ref: “I'll be glad to,” she answered. Then she quickly took the jar from her shoulder and held it while he drank.
text: Ní usapɔɔnee kíí-u yii: «Ukpil, m làá tīī-si á nyɔ̀.» Niinee ní u túutì u-baŋki malaa ní ki tīī-u nnyim u nyùn̄.

ref: After he had finished, she said, “Now I'll give your camels all the water they want.” She quickly poured out water for them, and she kept going back for more, until his camels had drunk all they wanted.
text: U lī mimmee ní ki kpáatì nnyim min kā kibaŋŋinee ki sītì laakin tiwaŋkuti nyùee ní ki tóotì ki cūtì ki gíti lúnní. U nìn gítí ki lúùń míǹ ní igutaamu kɔkɔ dāa nyùn̄ ki bál̀.

ref: Abraham's servant did not say a word, but he watched everything Rebekah did, because he wanted to know for certain if this was the woman the Lord had chosen.
text: Ní utɔntɔnlee kíǹ ki yú ki kpèé usapɔɔnee soooo ki dàkafī u-neen yii ti-Dindaan kalin bīiǹ u-saŋŋuee pu níii!

ref: The servant had brought along an expensive gold ring and two large gold bracelets. When Rebekah had finished bringing the water, he gave her the ring for her nose and the bracelets for her arms.
text: Igutaamu nyùn̄ ki dóòyee ní utɔntɔnlee lēē sindaariya bɔmbiki kin bi cáa tūuntí dimaŋfalee, kì nyìm̀ kii graambi biluu, ní ki lēē sindaariya bɔmbiliŋ ŋin bi cáa fūuntí iŋalee ali, ŋíǹ kpáfì ki nyìm̀ kii graambi kulaafaau ní ki dūu tīī usapɔɔnee.

ref: Then he said, “Please tell me who your father is. Does he have room in his house for me and my men to spend the night?”
text: Ní ki bí-u yii: «Ŋma bisal di sá-si ní? Ti ŋūǹ kí ká a-baa dumpu mpaan kí dòon̄aaa?»

ref: She answered, “My father is Bethuel, the son of Nahor and Milcah.
text: Ní usapɔɔnee kíí-u yii: «M-baa di sá Beetuweel, Naakɔɔr nì u-nimpuu Miilka jipɔɔn.»

ref: We have a place where you and your men can stay, and we also have enough straw and feed for your camels.”
text: Ní ki gíti bí-u yii: «Timool nì tifaal bí ti-dumpu tikpil á tīī a-gutaamui, mpaan mun bí ní dòon̄.»

ref: Then the servant bowed his head and prayed,
text: Niinee ní utɔntɔnlee gbāaǹ ki jáam̀ ti-Dindaan,

ref: “I thank you, Lord God of my master Abraham! You have led me to his relatives and kept your promise to him.”
text: ní ki bí yii: «M nyɔ́ŋkí ti-Dindaan, m-kpiliu Abraam Nimbɔtiu un ŋáań-u tiŋan múlmul kaa gbèentée! Úǹ di tí kpōotì-mi ki cáań ki kɔ́ǹ m-kpiliu nabiyaamu cindin táb.»

ref: Rebekah ran straight home and told her family everything.
text: Niinee ní Reebeeka sáǹ ki nín kùǹ ki cūtìi máafì tin jítèe kɔkɔ.

ref: Her brother Laban heard her tell what the servant had said, and he saw the ring and the bracelets she was wearing. So Laban ran out to Abraham's servant, who was standing by his camels at the well.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun Laaban ká kibɔmbiki kin bi cáa tūuntí dimaŋfalee, nì abɔmbil ŋin u-niisal fúuńee nì u nín tí gbìl̄ u-niisal nín máafì tin utɔntɔnlee sòon̄ nì uee, ní u nín cá utɔntɔnlee cee ki tin ká-u dinyimbɔbil caŋin, u yú ki kɔ́ĺ u-gutaamui.

ref: Then Laban said, “The Lord has brought you safely here. Come home with me. There's no need for you to keep on standing outside. I have a room ready for you in our house, and there's also a place for your camels.”
text: U bàn̄ee ní ki bí-u yii: «Dāan̄, sii un boon ti-Dindaan bīiǹee. Man bɔ́ntì a-diiku nì a-gutaamui dooŋu laŋki. Ba ŋá ní a gíti yú mmɔŋki?»

ref: Abraham's servant went home with Laban, where Laban's servants unloaded his camels and gave them straw and feed. Then they brought water into the house, so Abraham's servant and his men could wash their feet.
text: Ní utɔntɔnlee cūtì ki kɔ́ bi-dumpu, ní Laaban gútì igutaamu waŋguliŋ ní ki tīī-i timool nì tifaal, ní ki cáań nnyim ki tīī utɔntɔnlee nì bin nóoń-uee bí fíntí bi-taaŋi.

ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
text: U dóòyee ní ki cáań tijin ki yóoǹ-bi, ama ní utɔntɔnlee bí yii: «See ḿ sòoǹ tin m cááyee kí wàatī ní kí nín jī tijin.» Ní Laaban bí yii: «Too, án yaa sée, sòoǹ.»

ref: The servant explained: I am Abraham's servant.
text: Ní utɔntɔnlee bí yii: «Man sá Abraam tɔntɔnliu ní.

ref: The Lord has been good to my master and has made him very rich. He has given him many sheep, goats, cattle, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of silver and gold, and many slaves.
text: Ti-Dindaan bīiǹ m-kpiliu boon tiŋan ki yāntì u kpántì unikpaan. U tīī-u ipii nì iŋoobu nì inaa nì animbil nì sindaariya, ki tīī-u bitɔntɔnliibi bininjab nì binimpoobi, nì igutaamu nì mmamu.

ref: Sarah, my master's wife, didn't have any children until she was very old. Then she had a son, and my master has given him everything.
text: M-kpiliu nimpuu Saara màl̄-u kibininjabiki u-nimpunaatiiti poon, ní u dū u-gaajawanti tin u cááyee kɔkɔ ki tīī kibikee.

ref: I solemnly promised my master that I would do what he said. And he told me, “Don't choose a wife for my son from the women in this land of Canaan.
text: Ní m-kpiliu yāntì m pūtì ki pōoǹ-u. U nìn lī yii: <Taa cáá Kanaan yab bin kansikin m kóò nee sapɔmbi ubɔ kí tīī m-jipɔɔn.

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for my son from among my relatives.”
text: Ama, cù m-dumpu, m-cindi poon táb kí cáá usapɔɔn kí tīī m-jipɔɔn.>

ref: I asked my master, “What if the young woman refuses to come with me?”
text: Ní m bālifì-u yii: <Kin unimpu yaa kaa là kí nóoń-mi fáà!>

ref: My master answered, “I have always obeyed the Lord, and he will send his angel to help you find my son a wife from among my own relatives.
text: Ní u kíí-mi yii: <Ti-Dindaan un m nóóyee làá tɔ̄ń u-tuuŋŋu ŋú céeǹ-si a-saŋŋu ń còom̀ tiŋan, á ŋmā kí ká unimpu m-dumpu, m-cindi poon táb kí tīī m-jipɔɔn.>

ref: But if they refuse to let her come back with you, then you are freed from your promise.”
text: U gíti bí-mi yii: <A yaa cūtì m-dumpu, m-cindi poon ní baa kíí kí tīiń-si usapɔɔnee, a nyáń dipùtil din m yāntì a pūtèe bɔtin.>

ref: When I came to the well today, I silently prayed, “You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships, so please lead me to a wife for his son
text: Di sá m yíkìń ki dūu bāań díǹ nee dinyimbɔbil taapu ní ki bí yii: <Ti-Dindaan, m-kpiliu Abraam Nimbɔtiu, nsan ŋun m bí ki còom̄ nee, á tūtuǹ-mi!

ref: while I am here at the well. When a young woman comes out to get water, I'll ask her to give me a drink.
text: Kpèè, m bāań ki yú dinyimbɔbil nee caŋin. Usapɔɔn un yaa bāań kí lúń nnyim ní m bí-u yii: "M gbāam̀-si, pɔ̄-mi a-baŋki poon nnyim ḿ nyɔ̀ɔ,"

ref: If she gives me a drink and offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen.”
text: ní u yaa kíí-mi yii: "M làá tīī-si á nyɔ̀ kí lúń kí tīī a-gutaamui mun ń nyɔ̀ee," yàntī an sapɔɔŋwuee ń nīn sá unimpu un sii ti-Dindaan lēē kí tīī m-kpiliu jipɔɔnee.>

ref: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.
text: Maa láá dākafì míǹ m-pɔbilin ki dóò, ní Reebeeka páaǹní ki túń u-baŋki u-gbigbil pu ki dɔ́miń ki sīntì dinyimbɔbilin ki lúń nnyim, ní m mēē-u yii: <M gbāam̀-si, pɔ̄-mi nnyim ḿ nyɔ̀.>

ref: She quickly lowered the jar from her shoulder and said, “Have a drink. Then I'll get water for your camels.” So I drank, and after that she got some water for my camels.
text: Ní u túutì u-baŋki u-gbigbil pu malaa ki bí yii: <Nyɔ̀, a yaa nyùn̄ee ḿ lúń kí cáań a-gutaamui mun ń nyɔ̀.> U tīī-mi m nyùn̄ee ní ki lúń ki cáań m-gutaamui mun nyùn̄.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Ní m bālifì-u yii: <Ŋma bisal di sá-si ní?> Ní u bí yii: <M-baa di sá Beetuweel, Naakɔɔr nì u-nimpuu Miilka jipɔɔn.> Niinee ní m dū kibɔmbiki ki túuń u-maŋfal, ní ki dū abɔmbil ki fúuń u-ŋalii.

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: Ní ki gbāaǹ ki jáam̀ ti-Dindaan ní ki nyɔ́ŋkì úǹ m-kpiliu Abraam Nimbɔtiu, u nín kpōotì-mi ki tɔ́kɔǹ nsammɔnti pu m dāa bāań ki ká m-kpiliu maaliŋu poon usapɔɔn kí tīī u-jipɔɔnee.

ref: Now please tell me if you are willing to do the right thing for my master. Will you treat him fairly, or do I have to look for another young woman?
text: Kɔŋkɔnnee ni yaa là kí ŋá m-kpiliu tiŋan nì ibaamɔnee, ní kíí <iiin> àá <aayee>, ní án yaa sá ḿ túĺ ŋgiil puɔɔɔ, ŋgan puɔɔɔ, ḿ bée.»

ref: Laban and Bethuel answered, “The Lord has done this. We have no choice in the matter.
text: Ní Laaban nì Beetuweel bí-u yii: «Tibɔti nee poonee, timbi kaa ti pée cáá tiba kí lì, kun puee ti-Dindaan di yāntì an ŋá míǹ.

ref: Take Rebekah with you; she can marry your master's son, just as the Lord has said.”
text: Kpèè Reebeeka di sí a-nimbiin nee. Yòon̄-u kí cùnnì kí tīī a-kpiliu jipɔɔn ú cáá-u kii ti-Dindaan nín lī puee.»

ref: Abraham's servant bowed down and thanked the Lord.
text: Abraam tɔntɔnliu nín gbìl̄ an bɔtee ní u gbāaǹ ki jáam̀ ti-Dindaan.

ref: Then he gave clothing, as well as silver and gold jewelry, to Rebekah. He also gave expensive gifts to her brother and her mother.
text: U jáam̀ ti-Dindaan ki dóòyee ní ki nyántìń animbil kúti wanti, nì sindaariya wanti nì tiwampeeŋkaal ki tīī Reebeeka, ní ki tīī u-niija nì u-na mun tigaajawan tikpil.

ref: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
text: U ŋá míǹ kɔkɔ ki dóòyee ní u nì u-yab bin céeǹní-uee nín jíń ki nyùn̄ ní ki kɔ́ ki dōoǹ. Bi fīntì kutaafaauee ní u bí-bi yii: «M làá mèè-ni nsan kí nīn gítí m-kpiliu cee.»

ref: “Let Rebekah stay with us for a week or ten days,” they answered. “Then she may go.”
text: Ní usapɔɔn niija nì u-na bí-u yii: «Yàntī usapɔɔn ń gíti kàl̄ ti-cee iwiŋkaaŋkaa kii iwiin saalaa pu. Míǹ boonee ní á nín kpáaǹ-u kí búntí.»

ref: But he said, “Don't make me stay any longer. The Lord has already helped me find a wife for my master's son. Now let us return.”
text: Ní u kíí-bi yii: «Ti-Dindaan ŋáŋkì m-saŋŋu ki tīī-mi, ba ŋá ní ni gíti tàantí-mi? Pɔ̄maan-mi nsan ḿ nīn gítí m-kpiliu cee.»

ref: They answered, “Let's ask Rebekah what she wants to do.”
text: Ní bi bí yii: «An yaa sée, tí yíiń usapɔɔn u-ba kí bàlifì-u kí kpèè!»

ref: They called her and asked, “Are you willing to leave with this man at once?” “Yes,” she answered.
text: Ní bi lafun yíiń-u ki bālifì-u yii: «A làá nóó uninja neeee?» Ni u kíí-bi yii: «Iiin m làá nóó-u.»

ref: So they agreed to let Rebekah and an old family servant woman leave immediately with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Ní bi yāntì Laaban niisal Reebeeka nì unimpu un nìn lōō-uee yíkì ki nóó Abraam tɔntɔnliu nì bin céeǹní-uee ki búntì.

ref: They gave Rebekah their blessing and said, “We pray that God will give you many children and grandchildren and that he will help them defeat their enemies.”
text: Bi làá yīkèe ní Reebeeka yab mēē ki bīiǹ u-pu yii: «Sii ti-niisal, á kpántí nnibiim na. A-naantiibi ń dāá nīn pɔ̄ɔǹ bi-nannanliibi!»

ref: Afterwards, Rebekah and the young women who were to travel with her prepared to leave. Then they got on camels and left with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Bi dóòyee, ní Reebeeka yōoǹ nsapɔmbiyaamu mun bi tīī-uee, ní bi kāl̀ igutaamu pu ki páaǹ uninjee boon. Míǹ ní Abraam tɔntɔnliu yōoǹ Reebeeka bi kíĺ nsan ki nín kùǹ.

ref: At that time Isaac was living in the southern part of Canaan near a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Ŋyunti gbantee, Isaaki nìn nyánní laakin bi yíì Lakayi Rooyi lɔkɔuee ki gítiń, di sá u nìn kóò Neegeeb tiŋkin ní.

ref: One evening he was walking out in the fields, when suddenly he saw a group of people approaching on camels. So he started toward them. Rebekah saw him coming; she got down from her camel, and asked, “Who is that man?” “He is my master Isaac,” the servant answered. Then Rebekah covered her face with her veil.
text: ní ki bālifì utɔntɔnlee yii: «Ŋma di sá uninja un nyánní tisatin ki túŋkiń-ti nan ní?» Ní utɔntɔnlee kíí-u yii: «M-kpiliu dee.» Niinee ní u dū ŋŋaabiiŋkaa ki bīiǹ.

ref: The servant told Isaac everything that had happened.
text: Ní utɔntɔnlee máafì Isaaki tin kɔkɔ u ŋée.

ref: Isaac took Rebekah into the tent where his mother had lived before she died, and Rebekah became his wife. He loved her and was comforted over the loss of his mother.
text: Ní Isaaki yōoǹ Reebeeka ki cáa kɔ́ǹ u-na Saara tantiun, ní ki dū-u ki kpántì u-nimpuu ní ki néeń-u. An di ŋméeǹ Isaaki u-na kúm.

ref: and they had six sons: Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
text: U nìn màl̄-u Siimran nì Yɔkisan nì Meedan nì Madiyan nì Yiisbaaki nì Suuwa.

ref: Later, Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan, and when Dedan grew up, he had three sons: Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
text: Ní Yɔkisan màl̄ Seeba nì Deedan. Deedan naantitɔntɔkɔŋ di sá Asuur yab nì Leetusi yab nì Leewum yab.

ref: Midian also had five sons: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah.
text: Maadiyan jipɔmbi di sá Eeyifa nì Eefeer nì Anɔki nì Abidaa nì Eeldaa. Keetura jipɔmbi di sá bíǹ kɔkɔ.

ref: While Abraham was still alive, he gave gifts to the sons of Hagar and Keturah. He also sent their sons to live in the east far from his son Isaac, and when Abraham died, he left everything to Isaac. The Death of Abraham
text: ní ki pú u-nimpoobi bin u nìn gíti cáá boonee biyaamu mun tiwan tiba. Ŋyunti ŋun Abraam bí kaa láá kpíìyee ní u yāntì u-nimpoobee biyaamu yíkì ki búntì ndan, ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú, kí nīn dátiǹ u-jipɔɔn Isaaki. Abraam kúm

ref: His sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him east of Hebron in Machpelah Cave that was part of the field Abraham had bought from Ephron son of Zohar the Hittite. Abraham was buried there beside his wife Sarah.
text: ní Abraam nìn dá kusaau kun poon ntaŋkpalɔkɔ gbanti nìn bée. Niin ní bi nìn síb́ Abraam nì u-nimpuu Saara.

ref: God blessed Isaac after this, and Isaac moved to a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.” Ishmael's Descendants
text: Abraam kúm boonee, Unimbɔti nìn bīiǹ u-jipɔɔn Isaaki boon. U nìn kóò ki pìlīǹ Lakayi Rooyi lɔkɔu ní.

ref: Mishma, Dumah, Massa,
text: Miisima nì Duma nì Masa

ref: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
text: nì Adaadi nì Teema nì Yeetuur nì Nafiisi nì Keedima.

ref: Ishmael had settled in the land east of his brothers, and his sons settled everywhere from Havilah to Shur, east of Egypt on the way to Asshur. Ishmael was
text: Isimaayeel naantiibi nìn kóò kí yòon̄ Afiila tiŋki kí tin cáá sāā Suur tiŋki ní. Suur nìn bí ki pìlīǹ Eejipiti tiŋki, ní ki cá Asuur tiŋki watil pú. Niin ní bi nìn kóò. Bi nì bi-nabiyaamu Abraam maaliŋun yab bin kíǹee kaa nìn kpí. JAAKƆB (

ref: Isaac was the son of Abraham,
text: Abraam jipɔɔn Isaaki biyaamu mun u màl̄ee bɔti sèé. Abraam màl̄ Isaaki.

ref: Rebekah still had no children. So Isaac asked the Lord to let her have a child, and the Lord answered his prayer.
text: Reebeeka nìn sá uŋmaŋŋmanti ní Isaaki mēē ti-Dindaan u-pu, ní ti-Dindaan súń u-saai, ní u-nimpuu dāa cáá jaasib pool.

ref: Before Rebekah gave birth, she knew she was going to have twins, because she could feel them inside her, fighting each other. She thought, “Why is this happening to me?” Finally, she asked the Lord why her twins were fighting,
text: Mbiyaamuee bí mu-na poonee ní ki kpéetí tɔb, ní mu-na bí yii: «An yaa làá nīn bí mimmee, án làá kúntíǹ-mi mana dee?» Niinee ní u cūtì ki bālifì ti-Dindaan kun di yì-tì ní an ŋáań mimmee.

ref: and he told her: “Your two sons will become two separate nations. The younger of the two will be stronger, and the older son will be his servant.”
text: Ní ti-Dindaan bí-u yii: «Atimbun ali di bí a-poon, anibul ali làá dāá nyānní a-poon. Diba làá dāá nīn pɔ̄ɔǹ di-juti, ukpil di làá dāá nīn bí kiwaai taapu.»

ref: the first baby was covered with red hair, so he was named Esau.
text: Un u péé péé ki màl̄ee máǹ ní u-wunti cáá tikuti sùuu kii mpigbanee, ní u pú-u diyindi yii Eesayuu.

ref: The second baby grabbed on to his brother's heel, so they named him Jacob. Isaac was
text: Ní Eesayuu naal mun dāa nyánní ki ŋúbiń u-tagbindi ní bi pú-u diyindi yii Jaakɔb. Isaaki nìn cáá abin imuŋku ita ní ki màl̄-bi.

ref: As Jacob and Esau grew older, Esau liked the outdoors and became a good hunter, while Jacob lived the quiet life of a shepherd.
text: Ní bi dāa dūu wíiǹ ki ŋá binaacimpɔmbi. Eesayuu nìn dūu kpántì ukpan ki yáb̀, ki bí kumooun ki yìiǹ ki kpáŋkí, ní Jaakɔb úǹ nìn sá un bí sooooyee ki bí mmɔŋki.

ref: Esau would take the meat of wild animals to his father Isaac, so Isaac loved him more, but Jacob was his mother's favorite son.
text: Isaaki nìn là Eesayuu bɔti kun puee un nìn néeń tipeenan tin Eesayuu nìn cāabìńee. Ní Reebeeka úǹ là Jaakɔb bɔti.

ref: One day, when Jacob was cooking some stew, Esau came home hungry
text: Dalba Jaakɔb bíi ŋà tikunee, Eesayuu nyánní kusaau ki bāań, ki gīī púb.

ref: and said, “I'm starving to death! Here and now give me some of that red stew!” That's how Esau got the name “Edom.”
text: Ní u bí Jaakɔb yii: «An bàn̄-mi tikpil. M gbāam̀-si, pée jōō tikun tin a ŋāaǹ tì máǹ wèeee nee kí pɔ̄-mi ḿ wùfì, kun puee an bàn̄-mi tikpil.» An pu ní bi yíì-u yii Eedoom, an taapu sá yii <uman.>

ref: Jacob replied, “Sell me your rights as the first-born son.”
text: Ní Jaakɔb kíí-u yii: «Dū a-kpiti kí nyāfiǹ-mi díǹ».

ref: “I'm about to die,” Esau answered. “What good will those rights do me?”
text: Ní Eesayuu kíí yii: «Ŋkùm làá kpɔ̀-mi dee. Mì yaa kūū-mee, m làá gíti dū m-kpiti kí ŋá ba?»

ref: But Jacob said, “Promise me your birthrights, here and now!” And that's what Esau did.
text: Ní Jaakɔb bí-u yii: «Pùtī laakin ti bí nee díǹ kí wàatī.» Ní Eesayuu pūtì ní ki dū tikpiti ki nyáfiǹ Jaakɔb.

ref: Jacob then gave Esau some bread and some of the bean stew, and when Esau had finished eating and drinking, he just got up and left, showing how little he thought of his rights as the first-born.
text: Niinee ní Jaakɔb tīī Eesayuu kpɔnɔ nì atùmman kunti, ní u ŋmɔ́ ki wūfì tikun, ní ki yíkìi búntì. Eesayuu nín ŋá min ki kpēē ki sīŋkiǹ u-kpitee dee.

ref: Once during Abraham's lifetime, the fields had not produced enough grain, and now the same thing happened. So Isaac went to King Abimelech of the Philistines in the land of Gerar,
text: Abraam yuntiŋu ŋkùm nín nìn nyáń puee, boonee ŋkùm miba nìn gíti nyáń kitiŋ pu. Isaaki nìn cūtì Geeraar tiŋkin ki ká Fiiliistii yab bɔtiu Abimeeleeki.

ref: because the Lord had appeared to Isaac and said: Isaac, stay away from Egypt! I will show you where I want you to go.
text: Ní ti-Dindaan nyántì u-ba ki dàkā Isaaki ní ki bí-u yii: «Á taa cù Eejipiti. Nín̄ kā kitiŋ kin m làá dàkà-see poon.

ref: You will live there as a foreigner, but I will be with you and bless you. I will keep my promise to your father Abraham by giving this land to you and your descendants.
text: Á gbíntí kitiŋee poon, m làá nīn bí a-cee ki làá bìin̄ a-boon, kun puee sii nì a-naantiibi ní m làá dū kitiŋ gbanti kɔkɔ kí tīī. M làá ŋá míǹ kí dàkà yii m nín nìn pōoǹ min ki tīī a-baa Abraamee, maa làá kpántí.

ref: I will give you as many descendants as there are stars in the sky, and I will give your descendants all of this land. They will be a blessing to every nation on earth,
text: M làá yàntī a-naantiibi ń bùtī kii iŋmalbijaa in bí kutaa puee ní kí dū kitiŋ nee kɔkɔ kí tīī-bi. Sii maaliŋu pu ní m làá dī kí bìin̄ duulinya nib kɔkɔ boon,

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
text: Ní niin yab bālifì-u un ní Reebeeka sée. Ní u kíí-bi yii: «U sá m-niisal ní.» Waa nìn là kí lì yii Reebeeka sá u-nimpuu, kun puee u nìn fàŋkí bí taa kpɔ̀-u u-nimpuu Reebeeka pu, di sá yii u nìn ŋān̄ unimpu tikpil.

ref: After Isaac had been there a long time, King Abimelech looked out a window and saw Isaac hugging and kissing Rebekah.
text: Isaaki kāl̀ niin an yúntì. Dalbee Fiiliistii yab bɔtiu Abimeeleeki gbóoń tɔkɔla ki kpēēyee, Isaaki di bí ki gbíilíǹ u-nimpuu Reebeekee.

ref: Abimelech called him in and said, “Rebekah must be your wife! Why did you say she is your sister?” “Because I thought someone would kill me,” Isaac answered.
text: Ní u yíiń Isaaki ní ki bí-u yii: «Báà an ŋá pu a-nimpuu dee. Ba pu ní a tùkū-ti yii u sá a-niisal ní?» Ní Isaaki kíí-u yii: «Kun pu m lī mimmee di sá m fāŋkì yii bí taa kpɔ̀-mi u-pu ní.»

ref: “Don't you know what you've done?” Abimelech exclaimed. “If someone had slept with her, you would have made our whole nation guilty!”
text: Ní Abimeeleeki bí-u yii: «Ba pu ní a ŋá-ti míǹ? An kíǹ fiii m-nib kansikin ubɔ ń bàà dòon̄ nì a-nimpuu, ní á bàà yàntī tí kɔ́ tibɔtin.»

ref: Then Abimelech warned his people that anyone who even touched Isaac or Rebekah would be put to death.
text: Niinee ní Abimeeleeki kpáafì u-samaau kɔkɔ ki lī yii: «Un yaa pée gbēē Isaaki nì u-nimpuuee, see bí kpɔ̀ udaan.»

ref: Isaac planted grain and had a good harvest that same year. The Lord blessed him,
text: Dibindi gbantee Isaaki nìn kùn̄ tijin kitiŋ gbantin ní ki dāa cáń míǹ dilaataal pu, kun puee Unimbɔti nìn bīiǹ u-boon.

ref: and Isaac was so successful that he became very rich.
text: U nìn kpántì ŋŋmam daan ní u-gaajati mɔ̀tīi kútí ki cá ní u dāa dūu ŋá ugaajakpaan.

ref: In fact, the Philistines were jealous of the large number of sheep, goats, and slaves that Isaac owned,
text: U nìn cáá tipicaŋkpun nì tiŋɔcaŋkpun nì tinacaŋkpun nì bitɔntɔnliibi kupaau. Isaaki nì Abimeeleeki pōoǹ tɔb ipoon An nìn yāntì Fiiliistii yab kíǹ ki ná-u.

ref: and they stopped up the wells that Abraham's servants had dug before his death.
text: Ní Fiiliistii yab dū ntam ki gbíŋkì lɔkɔb bin kɔkɔ u-baa Abraam nìn yāntì u-tɔntɔnliibi gbìn̄ ŋyunti ŋun u dáá bée.

ref: Finally, Abimelech said, “Isaac, I want you to leave our country. You have become too powerful to stay here.”
text: Ní Abimeeleeki bí Isaaki yii: «A ká mpɔn tikpil ki jítiǹ-ti, míǹ puee yīkì ti-cee.»

ref: Isaac left and settled in Gerar Valley,
text: Ní Isaaki yíkì niin ki cūtìi kíǹ ki kóò Geeraar tantakalin.

ref: where he cleaned out those wells that the Philistines had stopped up. Isaac also gave each of the wells the same name that Abraham had given to them.
text: Ní Isaaki yāntì u-tɔntɔnliibi fātìi kɔ́tì lɔkɔb bin u-baa Abraam nìn dáá bí ki gbìn̄ kin u-kúm boon Fiiliistii yab gbíŋkèe. Ní u fātìi pú lɔkɔb gbanti ayin ŋin u-baa nìn pée yíì-bee.

ref: While his servants were digging in the valley, they found a spring-fed well.
text: Isaaki tɔntɔnliibi bíi gbì lɔkɔ ditantakalee poonee ní ki tin nyáń nnyimmɔntiim pu.

ref: But the shepherds of Gerar Valley quarreled with Isaac's shepherds and claimed the water belonged to them. So this well was named “Quarrel,” because they had quarreled with Isaac.
text: Ní Geeraar yab pikpaaliibi táká Isaaki pikpaaliibi ki lī yii: «Timbi di yì lɔkɔ nee nyim», an pu ní Isaaki pú lɔkɔ gbanti diyindi yii Eeseeki, taapu di sá yii <Bi kpákáǹ-mi.>

ref: Isaac's servants dug another well, and the shepherds also quarreled about it. So that well was named “Jealous.”
text: Ní u-tɔntɔnliibi gíti gbìn̄ lɔkɔ ubɔ ní bi tí dɔ́miń ki táká-bi, ní Isaaki pú lɔkɔ gbanti mun diyindi yii Siitina, taapu di sá yii <Bi jáń nì mi.>

ref: Finally, they dug one more well. There was no quarreling this time, and the well was named “Lots of Room,” because the Lord had given them room and would make them very successful.
text: Ní bi yíkì niin ki gíti tí cūtìi gbìn̄ laaba, ní baa ti táká-bi. Ní u pú lɔkɔ gbanti úǹ diyindi yii Reewoobooti, ki lī yii: «Ti-Dindaan yāntì ti tɔ́ ti-ba ní an bútì-ti kitiŋ nee poon.»

ref: Isaac went on to Beersheba,
text: Boonee ní u yíkì niin ki búntì Beerseeba.

ref: where the Lord appeared to him that night and told him, “Don't be afraid! I am the God who was worshiped by your father Abraham, my servant. I will be with you and bless you, and because of Abraham I will give you many descendants.”
text: Kunyeeu gbantee ní ti-Dindaan nyántì u-ba ki dàkā-u ní ki bí-u yii: «Man di sá a-baa Abraam Nimbɔtiu. Á taa fàŋkì, m bí a-cee. M làá bìin̄ a-boon kí bùtī a-maaliŋu m-tɔntɔnliu Abraam pu.»

ref: Isaac built an altar there and worshiped the Lord. Then he set up camp, and his servants started digging a well.
text: Ní Isaaki máń nsaraafan niin ki jáam̀ ti-Dindaan. Niin mun ní u gāā u-tantiu ní u-tɔntɔnliibi gbìn̄ lɔkɔ.

ref: Meanwhile, Abimelech had left Gerar and was taking his advisor Ahuzzath and his army commander Phicol to see Isaac.
text: Isaaki bí Beerseeba niinee ní ubɔti Abimeeleeki nì u-bɔɔ Awusati nì u-janjakpiliu Piikool nyáń doooo Geeraar ki cūtì u-cee.

ref: When they arrived, Isaac asked, “Why are you here? Didn't you send me away because you hated me?”
text: Ní Isaaki bí-bi yii: «Naa là m-bɔti ní ki jìn̄-mi ni-tiŋkin, ni gíti nyàab̀ ba m-cee doo?»

ref: They answered, “We now know for certain that the Lord is with you, and we have decided there needs to be a peace treaty between you and us. So let's make a solemn agreement
text: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Ti dáań ki ká yii ti-Dindaan bí a-cee. An pu ní ti ká nlandɔkɔ yii an máaǹ ti nì si ń kpāfì kí pùtī kí pòon̄ tɔb, mpèé ń nīn bí ti-kansikin.

ref: not to harm each other. Remember, we have never hurt you, and when we sent you away, we let you go in peace. The Lord has truly blessed you.”
text: Á pùtī yii aa làá dāá ŋá-ti tikpitil tiba kii ti mun nín kaa nìn ŋá-si tikpitil tiba puee. Ti nìn ŋá-si tiŋan ní ki yāntì a yíkì ti-cee nì ŋgbansɔŋfi. Kɔŋkɔnnee ti béè yii sii di sá un boon ti-Dindaan bīiǹee.»

ref: Isaac gave a big feast for them, and everyone ate and drank.
text: Niinee ní Isaaki bɔ́ntì jiŋaal ki fōō-bi kucaŋu, ní bi jíń ki nyùn̄.

ref: Early the next morning Isaac and the others made a solemn agreement, then he let them go in peace.
text: Ku wúntì kutaafaauee ní bi pūtì ki pōoǹ tɔb. Míǹ boonee ní Isaaki nyántì-bi nsanni, ní bi nín kùǹ nì dipɔɔsɔŋkil.

ref: Later that same day Isaac's servants came and said, “We've struck water!”
text: Ŋwiin gbanti ní Isaaki tɔntɔnliibi cáań-u lɔkɔ un bi bíi gbèe bɔti ki lī yii: «Ti gbìn̄ ki cíl̀ nnyim pu.»

ref: So Isaac named the well Shibah, and the town is still called Beersheba. Esau's Foreign Wives
text: Ní u pú lɔkɔ gbanti diyindi yii Siiba. An pu ní bi gbíntìi yíì kitiŋ gbanti díǹ pu nee yii Beerseeba, taapu di sá yii <dipùtil lɔkɔu.> Eesayuu nimpucáatam

ref: But these two women brought a lot of grief to Esau's parents Isaac and Rebekah.
text: Binimpoobi gbanti nìn yāntì Isaaki nì u-nimpuu Reebeeka nyɔmmu túŋkì.

ref: Isaac was old and almost blind, when he called in his first-born son Esau, who asked him, “Father, what can I do for you?”
text: Isaaki nìn púĺ ní u-nimbiliŋ pēefì waa ti wāĺ. Dalbee ní u yíiń u-jipɔŋkpiliu Eesayuu ní ki bí-u yii: «M-jipɔɔn bí laa!» Ní u kíí-u yii: «M-baa, m sèé.»

ref: Isaac replied, “I am old and might die at any time.
text: Ní Isaaki bí-u yii: «M púĺ ní nee, maa nyí m-kúm dal.

ref: So go hunting with your bow and arrows and kill a wild animal.
text: Míǹ puee, yòon̄ a-tɔbu nì a-baatandi nì a-kpáŋwanti kí cù kukpɔŋu kí kpɔ̀ń-mi upeel.

ref: Cook some of that tasty food that I love so much and bring it to me. I want to eat it once more and give you my blessing before I die.”
text: A yaa kūū ki bāańee, á ŋá tijin tin m pée néeń ki jīǹee kí cáań-mi ḿ jī. M yaa jíńee, ḿ mèè kí bìin̄ a-boon kí wàatī kí nín kpú.»

ref: Rebekah had been listening, and as soon as Esau left to go hunting,
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní Isaaki nìn bíi sòoǹ nì u-jipɔɔn Eesayuu mimmee, Reebeeka nìn bí ki pílifí. Eesayuu búntì kupeekpɔŋuee,

ref: she said to Jacob, “I heard your father tell Esau
text: ní Reebeeka bí Jaakɔb yii: «M gbìl̄ a-baa bíi tùkù a-maan yii:

ref: to kill a wild animal and cook some tasty food for him before he dies. Your father said this because he wants to bless your brother with the Lord as his witness.
text: <Cù kukpɔŋu kí cáań tipeenan kí ŋá tijin tin m pée néeń ki jīǹee, ḿ jī ní kí mèè kí bìin̄ a-boon ti-Dindaan nimbiin kí wàatī kí nín kpú.>

ref: Now, my son, listen carefully to what I want you to do.
text: An puee, m-jipɔɔn, pīlifì m nín làá tùkù-si puee kí ŋá míǹ.

ref: Go and kill two of your best young goats and bring them to me. I'll cook the tasty food that your father loves so much.
text: Á cù kuŋɔcaŋkpuŋun kí lēeń iŋɔpɔɔn ili kí cáań-mi ḿ ŋá tijin tin a-baa néeńee

ref: Then you can take it to him, so he can eat it and give you his blessing before he dies.”
text: á cáá tīī-u ú jī, ní kí mèè kí bìin̄ a-boon kí wàatī kí nín kpú.»

ref: “My brother Esau is a hairy man,” Jacob reminded her. “And I am not.
text: Ní Jaakɔb kíí u-na yii: «Kpèè, m-maan Eesayuu úǹ cáá tikuti ní man kaa cáá.

ref: If my father touches me and realizes I am trying to trick him, he will put a curse on me instead of giving me a blessing.”
text: An yaa yíì m-baa làá máań m-wunti kí kpèè. U yaa béè yii an sá mamee, m kpántì uŋmatinli dee u-cee. Mimmee m mēē mbusu ki tɔ́ m-ba pu dee, an kaa ti sá dibiindi.»

ref: Rebekah insisted, “Let his curse fall on me! Just do what I say and bring me the meat.”
text: Ní u-na bí-u yii: «Sii dee pīlifì man nín lī puee kí cùú cáań tin m bí yii á cáańee. Án yaa láa kpántì mbusuaaa, án gítí man pu.»

ref: So Jacob brought the meat to his mother, and she cooked the tasty food that his father liked.
text: Niinee ní Jaakɔb cūtìi cáań iŋoobuee ki tīī u-na, ní u-na dūu ŋá tijin tin u-baa néeń ki jīǹee.

ref: Then she took Esau's best clothes and put them on Jacob.
text: Reebeeka ŋá tijin ki dóòyee ní ki dū u-jipɔŋkpiliu Eesayuu bɔkutimɔntiŋ ŋin bí u-cee kudiinee ki dūu pēeǹ u-jipɔɔn uwaal Jaakɔb.

ref: She also covered the smooth part of his hands and neck with goatskins
text: Ní ki dū ŋŋɔgban ki fìn̄ u-ŋalii nì u-sil laakin dɔ̄ fámee.

ref: and gave him some bread and the tasty food she had cooked.
text: Ní ki dū tijin tin u ŋée nì kpɔnɔb bin u púĺee ki dūu ŋúbiǹ Jaakɔb ú cáá tīī u-baa.

ref: Jacob went to his father and said, “Father, here I am.” “Which one of my sons are you?” his father asked.
text: Jaakɔb bàn̄ee ní ki yíiń u-baa yii: «M-baa!» ní u-baa kíí-u yii: «M sèé. An sá sii m-jipɔɔn ŋma?»

ref: Jacob replied, “I am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.”
text: Ní Jaakɔb kíí-u yii: «An sá man a-jipɔŋkpiliu Eesayuu. Min a bālifì yii ḿ ŋá kí cáań-see ní m ŋá ki cáań. M gbāam̀-si, yīkìí kàl̄ kí jī tipeenan tin m cáańee jinti kí mèè kí bìin̄ m-boon.»

ref: Isaac asked, “My son, how did you find an animal so quickly?” “The Lord your God was kind to me,” Jacob answered.
text: Ní Isaaki bālifì u-jipɔɔn yii: «A ŋá mana ki ŋúń ki ká upeel malaa ki kūū míǹ?» Ní u kíí-u yii: «An sá ti-Dindaan a-Nimbɔtiu di yāntì m ká upeel malaa ki kūū.»

ref: “My son,” Isaac said, “come closer, where I can touch you and find out if you really are Esau.”
text: Ní Isaaki bí-u yii: «M-jipɔɔn, sútiń kí pìl̄-mi yéè ḿ máań-si kí kpèè yii a lafun sá m-jipɔɔn Eesayuu tiŋmanaaa?»

ref: Jacob went closer. His father touched him and said, “You sound like Jacob, but your hands feel hairy like Esau's.”
text: Ní u sútì ki pìl̄ u-baa ní u-baa máfì máfì-u, ní ki bí yii: «Nneen sá Jaakɔb neen, ama ní iŋal sá Eesayuu ŋalii.»

ref: Isaac asked, “Are you really my son Esau?” “Yes, I am,” Jacob answered.
text: Ní ki bālifì-u yii: «Kéè an sá sii m-jipɔɔn Eesayuu naa?» Ní Jaakɔb kíí-u yii: «Iiin.»

ref: So Isaac told him, “Serve me the wild meat, and I can give you my blessing.” Jacob gave him some meat, and he ate it. He also gave him some wine, and he drank it.
text: Ní u bí yii: «Too, m-jipɔɔn, cáań-mi upeel un a kūūyee jinti ḿ jī kí mèè kí bìin̄ a-boon.» Ní Jaakɔb cáań tijin ki yóoǹ-u u jíń, ní ki cáań-u ndaam u nyùn̄.

ref: Then Isaac said, “Son, come over here and kiss me.”
text: U jíń ki dóòyee ní ki bí Jaakɔb yii: «M-jipɔɔn, sútiń kí dāá kpàl̄-mi.»

ref: While Jacob was kissing him, Isaac caught the smell of his clothes and said: “The smell of my son is like a field the Lord has blessed.
text: Ní Jaakɔb sútì ki pìl̄ Isaaki ki kpàl̄-u, ní Isaaki núuń u-bɔkutil sɔnsɔfiŋu ní ki mēē ki bīiǹ u-boon ki lī yii: «Tiŋman, m-jipɔɔn sɔnsɔfiŋu bí kii kusaau kun pu ti-Dindaan bīiǹee ní.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: <Unimbɔti ń tīī a-kpaatanti nsɔŋfi ŋun nyēeń yilpuee, kí yàntī a-tiŋki ń nīn gàm̀ kí lòoǹ tiŋan, tikpaajin nì ndamam ń gbóotí a-cee.

ref: Nations will be your servants and bow down to you. You will rule over your brothers, and they will kneel at your feet. Anyone who curses you will be cursed; anyone who blesses you will be blessed.”
text: Anibul tikpil ń nīn sá a-tɔntɔnliibi, atimbun nib ń nīn gbàantí a-nimbiin. Á nīn sá a-nabiyaamu bɔtiu, a-naalib ń nīn gbàantí a-nimbiin! Un yaa mēē mbusu ki tɔ́ a-puee, án gítí udaan pu! Un yaa ŋá-si tiŋanee, udaan mun ń ká tiŋan!>»

ref: Right after Isaac had given Jacob his blessing and Jacob had gone, Esau came back from hunting.
text: Isaaki mēē ki bīiǹ Jaakɔb boon u nyáń kpalaayee, u-maan Eesayuu mun nyánní kukpɔŋu ki bāań dee.

ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
text: U mun bāańee ní ki ŋá tijin ki cáa cūnnì u-baa ní ki bí-u yii: «M-baa, yīkì kí jī tipeenan jinti tin ní man a-jipɔɔn ŋá ki cáań-see. A yaa jíńee, á mèè kí bìin̄ m-boon.»

ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: Ní u-baa bālifì-u yii: «An sá sii ŋma ní?» Ní u kíí yii: «An sá man a-jipɔŋkpiliu Eesayuu.»

ref: Isaac started trembling and said, “Then who brought me some wild meat right before you came in? I ate it and gave him a blessing that cannot be taken back.”
text: Niinee ní Isaaki wunti fáá dijinjikil ŋkpitim pu, ní u bí yii: «Kin ŋma di cūtì kukpɔŋu ki kūuń upeel ki ŋá tijin ki cáań-mi ní? M dáá pée jíń ki dóò ki mēē ki bīiǹ u-boon kpalaa ní sii mun dūu bāań nee. M nín mēē ki bīiǹ u-boon miŋyee, míǹ ní an làá ŋá kí tīī-u.»

ref: Isaac answered, “Your brother tricked me and stole your blessing.”
text: Ní u bí-u yii: «A-naal dɔ́miń ki kíil̀-mi ki fōō a-biindi.»

ref: Esau replied, “My brother deserves the name Jacob, because he has already cheated me twice. The first time he cheated me out of my rights as the first-born son, and now he has cheated me out of my blessing.” Then Esau asked his father, “Don't you have any blessing left for me?”
text: Ní Eesayuu bí yii: «Bi nín yíì-u yii Jaakɔb <Uŋmatinlee> pu ní u líbitì-mi míǹ mili kɔkɔɔɔ? U nìn fōō m-kpiti, ní kɔŋkɔnnee u tí fōō m-biindi. Mimmee, m-baa, aa kíǹ-mi dibiindi dibaaa?»

ref: “My son,” Isaac answered, “I have made Jacob the ruler over you and your brothers, and all of you will be his servants. I have also promised him all the grain and grapes that he needs. There's nothing left that I can do for you.”
text: Ní u-baa fātìi kíí-u yii: «Kpèè, úǹ ní m dūu ŋá a-kpiliu, ní ki dū u-nabiyaamu kɔkɔ ki ŋá u-tɔntɔnliibi, ní ki bí yii ú nīn cáá tikpaajin nì ndamam tikpil, kin ba di gíti bí m ŋūǹ kí ŋá kí tīī-si, m-jipɔɔn!»

ref: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
text: Ní Eesayuu bí u-baa yii: «Mimmee dibiindi nee baba di pée bí a-ceeee, m-baa? Bìin̄ ma mun pu, m-baa!» U lī mimmee ní ki bíi sūǹ mpɔn.

ref: So his father said: “Your home will be far from that fertile land, where dew comes down from the heavens.
text: Ní u-baa bí yii: «Too, kpèè laakin sii làá nīn kóòyee, tijin làá nīn kpá niin. Kitiŋ kaa làá nīn cáá nsɔŋfi ŋun nyēeń yilpuee.

ref: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”
text: Sii baatandi pu ní a làá nīn jīǹ. A làá nīn bí a-naal taapu ní. Ama a yaa láa pɔ́ɔǹ ki fōō a-bee ní a dāa léetí a-neen kpancikiliu.» Jaakɔb yíkì ki cá u-wee Laaban cee

ref: Esau hated his brother Jacob because he had stolen the blessing that was supposed to be his. So he said to himself, “Just as soon as my father dies, I'll kill Jacob.”
text: Niinee Eesayuu kíǹ ki ná Jaakɔb dibiindi din u fōō bi-baa ceeyee pu, ní ki lì u-neen yii: «M-baa kúm kaa ti dátì. U yaa kpíìyee, see ḿ kpɔ̀ m-naal Jaakɔb.»

ref: When Rebekah found out what Esau planned to do, she sent for Jacob and told him, “Son, your brother Esau is just waiting for a chance to kill you.
text: Ní bi dɔ́miń ki tùkū Reebeeka tin u-jipɔŋkpiliu Eesayuu bí ki lì yii u làá ŋée. Niinee ní u yíiń u-jipɔɔn uwaal Jaakɔb ní ki bí-u yii: «Kpèè, a-maan Eesayuu là kí kpɔ̀-si kí tɔ̀ ipɔntoon.

ref: Now listen carefully and do what I say. Go to the home of my brother Laban in Haran
text: An puee m-biki, á pīlifì m nín làá sāŋkì-si puee! Yīkìí sāǹ kí nīn cá m-niija Laaban cee, Kaaran tiŋkin.

ref: and stay with him for a while. When Esau stops being angry
text: Á nīn bí niin kí dāá sāā ŋyunti ŋun a-maan ŋuul làá dāá sɔ̀ŋkèe.

ref: and forgets what you have done to him, I'll send for you to come home. Why should I lose both of my sons on the same day?”
text: U yaa láa súntì tin a ŋá-u u-ŋuul sɔ̄ŋkèe, m làá dāá tɔ̄ bí yíiń-si á gítiń doo. M yaa kaa ŋá míǹ, m làá dāá lóó ni-liitil nee ŋwimbaanti ní.»

ref: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
text: Ní Reebeeka bí Isaaki yii: «M-naŋki nyáń nì m-ba Eeti yab nimpubiyaamu nee pu. Jaakɔb mun yaa gíti cáá kitiŋ nee poon unimpu kii bin bí neeyee, man yaa pūtì kpíì an sɔ́!»

ref: Isaac called in Jacob, then gave him a blessing, and said: Don't marry any of those Canaanite women.
text: Ní Isaaki yíiń Jaakɔb ní ki mēē ki bīiǹ u-boon ní ki yóoǹ-u mara nee yii: «A taa pée cáá Kanaan tiŋkin doo unimpu.»

ref: Go at once to your mother's father Bethuel in northern Syria and choose a wife from one of the daughters of Laban, your mother's brother.
text: Ní ki bí-u yii: «Yīkì kí nīn cá Padan-Aram, a-na baa Beetuweel cee kí kpèè a-wee Laaban sapɔmbi kansikin ubɔ kí cáá.

ref: I pray that God All-Powerful will bless you with many descendants and let you become a great nation.
text: Unimbɔti Mpɔŋkɔdaan ń bìin̄ a-boon á màal̀ kí bùtī, a-maaliŋu ń kpántí atimbun kupaau.

ref: May he bless you with the land he promised Abraham, so that you will take over this land where we now live as foreigners.
text: Ú dū tiŋan tin u nìn pōoǹ Abraamee kí tīī sii nì a-naantiibi, án nín làá ŋá pu á dāá nīn kíǹ ki yì kitiŋ kin poon a kóò neeyee, an dàkà yii kitiŋ kin ní Unimbɔti nìn dūu tīī Abraamee!»

ref: Isaac then sent Jacob to stay with Rebekah's brother Laban, the son of Bethuel the Aramean. Esau Marries the Daughter of Ishmael
text: Ní Isaaki yāntì Jaakɔb nín cá Padan-Aram, Beetuweel uaranja jipɔɔn Laaban cee. Laaban gbanti di sá Jaakɔb nì Eesayuu na Reebeeka niija.

ref: Esau found out that his father Isaac had blessed Jacob and had warned him not to marry any of the Canaanite women. He also learned that Jacob had been sent to find a wife in northern Syria
text: Eesayuu nìn ká yii Isaaki mēē ki bīiǹ Jaakɔb boon ki bí yii ú nīn cá Padan-Aram kí cáá unimpu niin, ní ki tīī-u mara yii ú taa cáá unimpu Kanaan yab sapɔmbi kansikin.

ref: and that he had obeyed his father and mother.
text: U nìn gíti ká yii Jaakɔb nìn gbìl̄ u-baa nì u-na nyɔkɔ ní ki búntì Padan-Aram.

ref: Esau already had several wives, but he now realized how much his father hated the Canaanite women.
text: Niinee ní Eesayuu béè yii u-baa kaa là Kanaan sapɔmbi bɔti.

ref: So he married Ishmael's daughter Mahalath, who was the sister of Nebaioth and the granddaughter of Abraham. Jacob's Dream at Bethel
text: An pu ní u cūtì Abraam jipɔɔn Isimaayeel cee ki cáá úǹ bisal Maalaati, Neebayɔti niisal ki kútì u-nimpoobi bin u pée cááyee pu. Jaakɔb damintiiku

ref: Jacob left the town of Beersheba and started out for Haran.
text: Ní Jaakɔb yíkì Beerseeba tiŋkin ki búntì Kaaran tiŋkin.

ref: At sunset he stopped for the night and went to sleep, resting his head on a large rock.
text: U bàn̄ laabee, kunyeeu mɔ́-u ní u gbíntì niin ki dōoǹ, ní ki yōoǹ ditaŋkpal diba ki kúĺ u-yil.

ref: In a dream he saw a ladder that reached from earth to heaven, and God's angels were going up and down on it.
text: U dɔ̄ee ní ki dámintì yii akɔl yú kitiŋ ki tin sāā kutagbɔŋu ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi tà ki jɔ́ḿ ki kpákatiń.

ref: The Lord was standing beside the ladder and said: I am the Lord God who was worshiped by Abraham and Isaac. I will give to you and your family the land on which you are now sleeping.
text: U kpēēyee, ti-Dindaan yú ki kɔ́ĺ-u ní ki bí-u yii: «Man di sá a-Dindaan a-naanja Abraam Nimbɔtiu nì a-baa Isaaki Nimbɔtiu. Kitiŋ kin pu a dɔ̄ nee, m làá dāá dū-kì kí tīī sii nì a-naantiibi ní.

ref: Your descendants will spread over the earth in all directions and will become as numerous as the specks of dust. Your family will be a blessing to all people.
text: A-naantiibi làá dāá nīn wīikí kii kitiŋ pu bulee ní. Bi dāa kpátí kí nīn cá ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú nì ŋu-jeetilaŋki pú, kí nīn cá ŋgan pú nì ŋgiil pú. Sii nì a-naantiibi pu ní m làá dāá ŋá kitiŋ kɔkɔ nib tiŋan.

ref: Wherever you go, I will watch over you, then later I will bring you back to this land. I won't leave you—I will do all I have promised.
text: Á béé yii m làá nīn bí a-cee, báà laakin ní a yaa bée, ki làá nīn cí-si, ní kí dāá fàtīí gíiǹní-si kitiŋ nee poon. M kpá kí fá-si kí tin cáá sāā ŋyunti ŋun m làá dāá ŋá tin kɔkɔ m tùkū-si nee kí tīī-see.»

ref: Jacob woke up suddenly and thought, “The Lord is in this place, and I didn't even know it.”
text: Jaakɔb fīntèe ní ki bí yii: «Tiŋman, ti-Dindaan di bí doo ní man kaa nyí.»

ref: Then Jacob became frightened and said, “What a frightening place! It must be the house of God and the gateway to heaven.”
text: U fāŋkì ní ki lī yii: «Mpaan nee kaa cáá tinaacin. Doo di sá Unimbɔti diiku tiŋman, yilpu punyɔkɔki dee.»

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Before that it had been named Luz.
text: Ní ki pú niin diyindi yii Beeteel, ama bi nìn dáá yíì kitiŋ gbanti Luusi ní.>

ref: Jacob solemnly promised God, “If you go with me and watch over me as I travel, and if you give me food and clothes
text: Ní Jaakɔb pūtì ki pōoǹ yii: «Unimbɔti, a yaa bí m-cee ki yaa bīiǹ m-boon m-saŋŋu ŋun m cá nee poon, ki yaa yāntì m ká ki jíń ki ká ki pēeǹ,

ref: and bring me safely home again, you will be my God.
text: ki ŋúń ki fātìi gítìń m-baa cindi nì ŋgbampɔnee, sii ti-Dindaan di dāa kíǹ ki sá man Nimbɔtiu.

ref: This rock will be your house, and I will give back to you a tenth of everything you give me.”
text: Ditaŋkpal din m dūu cóoǹ bí nīn téetí nee di dāa sá sii Unimbɔti diiku. Tin kɔkɔ a yaa láa tīī-mee, m làá dāá lēē saalaa kamaa pu kubɔ kubɔ kí tīī-si.»

ref: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
text: Ní Jaakɔb yíkì ki nìkī u-saŋŋu ki túĺ ntimu mun bí ŋwìiń nyalaŋki púee.

ref: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
text: U kpēēyee, lɔkɔ ubɔ dɔ̄ akpaanni tipicaŋkpun tita dɔ̄ ki kɔ́ĺ. Niin ní bipikpaaliibi nìn lúù nnyim ki nyùntī ipii. Ditaŋkpakpaandi diba nìn pīī lɔkɔee nyɔkɔpu.

ref: Shepherds would roll the rock away when all their sheep had gathered there. Then after the sheep had been watered, the shepherds would roll the rock back over the mouth of the well.
text: Míǹ puee bi nìn cīití ipii kɔkɔ yaa kpáfì ki dóòyee ní bí nín bíntí ditaŋkpalee lɔkɔ nyɔkɔ ní kí nyùntī i-kɔkɔ. I yaa nyùn̄ ki dóòyee ní bí fàtīí bíntí ditaŋkpalee kí pìin̄ lɔkɔee nyɔkɔ.

ref: Jacob asked the shepherds, “Where are you from?” “We're from Haran,” they answered.
text: Ní Jaakɔb bālifì bipikpaaliibee yii: «M-nabiyaamu, ni sá la ní?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Ti sá Kaaran yab ní.»

ref: Then he asked, “Do you know Nahor's grandson Laban?” “Yes we do,” they replied.
text: Ní u bālifì-bi yii: «Ni nyí Laaban, Naakɔɔr naantiiuaaa?» Ni bi kíí-u yii: «Iiin, ti nyí-u.»

ref: “How is he?” Jacob asked. “He's fine,” they answered. “And here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”
text: Ní u bālifì-bi yii: «U pɔ̄ɔ̄ tiŋanaaa?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Alaafiya bí-u. Kpèè, u-bisal Raseel di dòoń nì ipii nee.»

ref: Jacob told them, “Look, the sun is still high up in the sky, and it's too early to bring in the rest of the flocks. Water your sheep and take them back to the pasture.”
text: Ni u bí-bi yii: «Ŋwìiń dáá bí, an kaa láá ŋmāntì ŋyunti ŋun bi kpáfí ipiiyee. Míǹ puee ní lúń nnyim kí nyùntī-i, ni yaa nyūntì-iee ní gíiǹ-i kupikpaaun.»

ref: But they replied, “We can't do that until they all get here, and the rock has been rolled away from the well.”
text: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Taa ŋūǹ ki ŋá míǹ, asee ipii kɔkɔ yaa bāań ki kpáfì. Niinee ní tí nín cúú ditaŋkpal kí bíntí ní kí nyùntī-i.»

ref: While Jacob was still talking with the men, his cousin Rachel came up with her father's sheep.
text: Bi yú ki sòoǹ míǹ ní Raseel dūu bāań nì u-baa piii, di sá Raseel nìn sá upikpaal ní.

ref: When Jacob saw her and his uncle's sheep, he rolled the rock away and watered the sheep.
text: Jaakɔb nín ká u-wee Laaban bisal Raseel péetiń u-baa piiiyee, ní u dɔ́miń ki cúú ditaŋkpalee ki bíntì ní ki nyūntì-i.

ref: He then kissed Rachel and started crying because he was so happy.
text: Ní Jaakɔb kpàl̄ Raseel ní ki bíi sūǹ nì dipɔɔpindi.

ref: He told her that he was the son of her aunt Rebekah, and she ran and told her father about him.
text: Ní ki bí Raseel yii: «M sá a-baa niisal Reebeeka jipɔɔn ní.» Ní Raseel sáǹ ki cūtì ki tùkū u-baa.

ref: As soon as Laban heard the news, he ran out to meet Jacob. He hugged and kissed him and brought him to his home, where Jacob told him everything that had happened.
text: Laaban nín gbìl̄ yii u-niisal jipɔɔn bāańee, ní u sáǹ ki túŋkì-u. U bàn̄ee, ní ki kpàl̄-u ki nákáǹ u-ba, ní ki cáá-u ki cūnnì mmɔŋki. Ní Jaakɔb máafì-u tin jítèe kɔkɔ.

ref: Laban said, “You are my nephew, and you are like one of my own family.” Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel After Jacob had been there for a month,
text: Ní Laaban bí Jaakɔb yii: «Tiŋman, a baasii sá m-niliu ní kiŋkiŋ.» Jaakɔb nìn kāl̀ Laaban cee ki sāā uŋmal ubɔ. Jaakɔb cáá Leeya nì Raseel

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: Dalbee ní Laaban bí Jaakɔb yii: «A nín sá m-niliuee puee, á nīn tūǹ ditundi míǹ faalaa ki tìì-maaa? An puee tùkù-mi kun a là ḿ dāá pà-see.»

ref: Laban had two daughters. Leah was older than Rachel, but her eyes didn't sparkle, while Rachel was beautiful and had a good figure.
text: Leeya kaa nìn cáá kunyaaŋŋu, ama ní Raseel úǹ nìn bí gbàlgbal ki ŋān̄ tikpil.

ref: Since Jacob was in love with Rachel, he answered, “If you will let me marry Rachel, I'll work seven years for you.”
text: Jaakɔb nìn néeń Raseel, ní ki bí u-wee yii: «M làá tɔ̄ ditundi kí tīī-si abin aluli á dū a-bisal uwaal Raseel kí tīī-mi.»

ref: Laban replied, “It's better for me to let you marry Rachel than for someone else to have her. So stay and work for me.”
text: Ní Laaban bí-u yii: «An mɔ̄ǹ-mi ḿ dū m-bisal kí tīī sii ki jítiǹ ḿ dū-u kí tīī uniyaŋyan. Nín̄ kā m-cee.»

ref: Jacob worked seven years for Laban, but the time seemed like only a few days, because he loved Rachel so much.
text: Ní Jaakɔb túń ditundi abin aluli Raseel pu ní an kaa kpáàa súŋkì u-cee, kun puee u nìn néeń-u tikpil.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Niinee ní Jaakɔb bí Laaban yii: «M túń ditundi ki sāā m-yuntiŋu, an puee dū m-nimpuu ki tīī-mi ḿ cáá.»

ref: So Laban gave a big feast and invited all their neighbors.
text: Ní Laaban kóoǹ niin yab kɔkɔ ki kpáfì ní bi jíń tinimpucan jiŋaaliu.

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.
text: Kunyeeuee ní Laaban yōoǹ u-biseebiki Leeya ki cáa kɔ́ǹ Jaakɔb cee ní u nì Jaakɔb kpáfì.

ref: Laban also gave Zilpah to Leah as her servant woman.
text: Ní Laaban dū u-yumbusapɔɔŋwu Siilpa ki tīī u-bisal Leeya ú nīn bí u-cee ki tūǹ ditundi ki tìì-u.

ref: The next morning Jacob found out that he had married Leah, and he asked Laban, “Why did you do this to me? Didn't I work to get Rachel? Why did you trick me?”
text: Kutaa wúntèe, Jaakɔb kpēēyee Leeya dee, ní an ŋá-u bitii. Ní u bālifì u-wee Laaban yii: «Ba ŋá ní a ŋá-mi mimmee? An kaa sá Raseel pu ní m kāl̀ a-cee ki túń ditundi ki tīī-saaa? Ba pu ní a kíil̀-mi ki dū Leeya ki tīī-mi?»

ref: Laban replied, “In our country the older daughter must get married first.
text: Ní Laaban kíí-u yii: «Timbi cee dooyee, an sá-ti dikoobil ní, uwaal kaa pēentí ukpil kí mɔ̄ǹ.

ref: After you spend this week with Leah, you may also marry Rachel. But you will have to work for me another seven years.”
text: Míǹ puee púntí sii nì un bí nee nimpucanti kinyɔŋ nee, kin dāa bíńee, ḿ dū Raseel mun kí tīī-si, á tí kàl̄ abin aluli kí tɔ̄ ditundi kí tīī-mi.»

ref: At the end of the week of celebration, Laban let Jacob marry Rachel, and he gave her his servant woman Bilhah. Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, but he had to work another seven years for Laban.
text: Ní Jaakɔb nì Raseel mun kpáfì. Jaakɔb nìn néeń Raseel ki jítiǹ Leeya, ní ki nìn gíti kāl̀ abin aluli ki túń ditundi ki tīī Laaban. Jaakɔb biyaamu bɔti

ref: The Lord knew that Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, and so he gave children to Leah, but not to Rachel.
text: Ti-Dindaan nín ká yii Jaakɔb kaa néeń Leeyayee, ní u pú-u mmaal, ní Raseel úǹ sá uŋmaŋŋmanti.

ref: Leah gave birth to a son and named him Reuben. Then she said, “The Lord has taken away my sorrow. Now my husband will love me more than he does Rachel.”
text: Leeya nìn dūu cáá dipool ki màl̄ kibininjabiki, ní ki pú-kì diyindi yii Rubeen. Di sá yii un nìn lī yii: «Ti-Dindaan ká m-falaau, kɔŋkɔnnee m-cal làá nīn kíǹ ki néeń-mi.»

ref: She had a second son and named him Simeon, because she said, “The Lord has heard that my husband doesn't love me.”
text: Ní u gíti cáá dipool ki màl̄ kibininjabiki, ní ki bí yii: «Ti-Dindaan nín ká yii m-cal kaa néeń-mee ní u gíti pú-mi kibininjabiki kiba.» Ní u pú-kì diyindi yii Simeeyɔn, taapu di sá yii: <Ti-Dindaan gbìl̄.>

ref: Rachel was very jealous of Leah for having children, and she said to Jacob, “I'll die if you don't give me some children!”
text: Raseel nín ká yii waa bíi màal̀ Jaakɔb mbiyaamuee, ní u kíǹ ki ŋáań u-maan ipoompoon, ní ki bí Jaakɔb yii: «Yàntī ḿ màal̀, an yaa kaa sá míǹ m làá kpú.»

ref: But Jacob became upset with Rachel and answered, “Don't blame me! I'm not God.”
text: Ní Jaakɔb fōō u-pu diŋuul ní ki bí yii: «Man di sá Unimbɔti un kaa dū mmaal ki ŋá a-poon á nīn màal̀eeee?»

ref: “Here, take my servant Bilhah,” Rachel told him. “Have children by her, and I'll let them be born on my knees to show that they are mine.”
text: Niinee ní Raseel bí Jaakɔb yii: «Kpèè, m-yumbusapɔɔŋwu Biila sɔ̀. Dū-u kí kpántí a-nimpuu ú màal̀ kí tīī-mi, ní u-puee ma mun ń ká kibiki.»

ref: and they had a son.
text: Ní Biila cáá dipool ní ki màl̄ Jaakɔb kibininjabiki.

ref: Rachel named him Dan, because she said, “God has answered my prayers. He judged in my favor and has given me a son.”
text: Ní Raseel bí yii: «Unimbɔti pílifiǹ-mi, ki pɔ̀n̄-mi ki tīī-mi kibininjabiki.» An pu ní u pú-kì diyindi yii Daani, taapu di sá yii: «U pɔ̀n̄-mi.»

ref: When Bilhah and Jacob had a second son,
text: Ní Biila gíti cáá dipool ní ki màl̄ Jaakɔb kibininjabiki kililiitii.

ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Ní Raseel bí yii: «M mɔ̀n̄ nì m-maan Unimbɔti nimbiin ki pɔ́ɔkiǹ-u.» Ní ki pú-kì diyindi yii Neefitalii, taapu di sá yii <M-mool.>

ref: When Leah realized she could not have any more children, she let Jacob marry her servant Zilpah,
text: Leeya mun kpēē yii waa ti bíi màal̀ee, ní u dū u-yumbusapɔɔŋwu Siilpa ki tīī Jaakɔb ú dūú kpántí u-nimpuu.

ref: and they had a son.
